_meta.purpose: Bacteriology — Gram stain (+ acid-fast) morphotype -> likely organisms & significance. Keys are YOLO class names from the bacteriology detector. Decision support only; culture + ID/AST confirm. Report morphology + arrangement, not a definitive organism, from the smear.
_meta.source: Standard clinical microbiology (Koneman; CLSI). Curated for JORINOVA NEXUS.

gram_positive_cocci_clusters.name: Gram-positive cocci in clusters
gram_positive_cocci_clusters.note: Clusters suggest Staphylococcus. Correlate with catalase/coagulase; MRSA risk.

gram_positive_cocci_chains.name: Gram-positive cocci in chains
gram_positive_cocci_chains.note: Chains suggest Streptococcus. Group A (S. pyogenes), viridans, Enterococcus.

gram_positive_cocci_pairs.name: Gram-positive diplococci (lancet)
gram_positive_cocci_pairs.note: Lancet-shaped diplococci in CSF/sputum -> pneumococcus (urgent in CSF).

gram_negative_bacilli.name: Gram-negative bacilli (rods)
gram_negative_bacilli.note: Commonest in UTI/sepsis. In blood culture -> notify; ESBL/CRE risk.

gram_negative_diplococci.name: Gram-negative diplococci
gram_negative_diplococci.note: Intracellular GN diplococci in CSF -> MENINGOCOCCUS: medical emergency, notify immediately.

gram_positive_bacilli.name: Gram-positive bacilli (rods)
gram_positive_bacilli.note: Consider Listeria in CSF/neonates; Clostridium if anaerobic/gas.

gram_variable_bacilli.name: Gram-variable bacilli
gram_variable_bacilli.note: Clue cells + GV rods in vaginal smear -> bacterial vaginosis.

acid_fast_bacilli.name: Acid-fast bacilli (Ziehl-Neelsen)
acid_fast_bacilli.note: CRITICAL: TB is notifiable — start airborne isolation, confirm GeneXpert/culture, grade AFB load.

yeast.name: Yeast / budding yeast (Gram)
yeast.note: Correlate with pus cells; candiduria/candidaemia risk in at-risk hosts.

pus_cell.name: Pus cell (neutrophil)
pus_cell.note: Many pus cells + organisms + few epithelials = good specimen with infection.
